text.title: Product Development Trends Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Product Development Trends methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every trend MUST cite >=3 sources spanning >=3 classes (academic/filing, practitioner blog, market signal).
Score every trend on signal strength + adoption velocity + revenue alignment + decay risk; each 1-5.
rule.rationale: Single-axis trend scoring (hype) misses decay timing.

rule.statement: Every trend MUST receive a bet | monitor | ignore verdict; no 'maybe'.
rule.rationale: Maybes accumulate; explicit verdicts force the decision.

rule.statement: Every 'bet' verdict MUST carry a kill criterion (signal X drops below Y => abort).
rule.rationale: Bets without exit criteria become sunk-cost projects.

rule.statement: Trend briefs are quarterly artefacts; do not author monthly (sample size too small) or annually (already stale).
rule.rationale: Quarterly cadence balances signal accumulation with relevance window.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
